Member.title: Member
Member.description: Represents a member participating in the Chit Fund. Members can have either monthly or daily contribution plans, each with specific details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Member entity.

name.description: The full name of the member.

phone.description: The contact phone number of the member.

joinDate.description: The date and time when the member joined the chit fund.

collectionType.description: Specifies the type of collection plan for this member ('monthly' or 'daily').

monthlyContributionAmount.description: The fixed amount the member contributes monthly. Applicable when 'collectionType' is 'monthly'.

monthlyDueDate.description: The day of the month (1-31) when the monthly contribution is due. Applicable when 'collectionType' is 'monthly'.

dailyContributionAmount.description: The fixed amount the member contributes daily. Applicable when 'collectionType' is 'daily'.

status.description: The current operational status of the member in the chit fund ('active' or 'inactive').

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records individual payment transactions made by members towards their contributions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

memberId.description: Reference to the Member who made this payment. (Relationship: Member 1:N Payment)

amountPaid.description: The actual amount paid in this transaction.

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was recorded.

paymentType.description: Indicates if this payment is for a 'monthly' or 'daily' collection. This aligns with the member's collectionType.

periodCovered.description: A string identifying the specific period this payment covers. For 'monthly' payments, it should be in 'YYYY-MM' format (e.g., '2023-10'). For 'daily' payments, it should be in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format (e.g., '2023-10-26'). This is crucial for matching payments to obligations.

ChitRound.title: ChitRound
ChitRound.description: Stores information about each completed chit fund round, including the winner and amount.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChitRound entity.

roundNumber.description: The sequential number of the chit round.

winnerMemberId.description: Reference to the Member who won this chit round. (Relationship: Member 1:N ChitRound)

winningAmount.description: The amount won by the member in this round.

roundDate.description: The date and time when this chit round concluded and the winner was declared.

definition.description: Stores details for each member, including their collection plan type and contribution amounts. This collection will now include the 'monthlyDueDate' field for members with a 'monthly' collectionType. Authorization for this collection is controlled by the 'roles_admin' collection; only authenticated admins can manage members.

params.name: memberId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific member.

definition.description: Records individual payment transactions made by members. Documents contain 'memberId', 'paymentType' (monthly/daily), and 'periodCovered' (YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD), which are crucial for the application to derive payment statuses. Authorization is restricted to authenticated admins, based on the 'roles_admin' collection.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores information about each completed chit fund round, including winner details and amounts. Authorization for this collection is controlled by the 'roles_admin' collection; only authenticated admins can manage chit round records.

params.name: roundId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific chit fund round.

definition.description: This collection implements Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document where the ID matches `request.auth.uid` grants administrator privileges across the application. This ensures Authorization Independence, as security rules can atomically check for admin status without referencing other documents. No specific schema beyond the UID being the document ID is required.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator user. The presence of a document at this path grants admin access.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the enhanced monthly collection logic, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The primary goal is to add a 'monthlyDueDate' field for monthly members and update the payment status calculation for monthly contributions without affecting the existing daily logic or legacy data. Authorization independence is achieved through a dedicated 'roles_admin' collection, where the mere existence of an admin's UID grants broad access to all application data. This eliminates the need for 'get()' operations in security rules, ensuring atomic authorization checks and robust security. For instance, any request from an authenticated user will first check if their `uid` exists in the `/roles_admin` collection. If it does, they are granted read/write access to relevant collections like `/members`, `/payments`, and `/chitRounds`. This makes authorization highly debuggable and secure.

Structural segregation is maintained by distinguishing members by their 'collectionType' field within the `/members` collection. Although payments for both daily and monthly types reside in a single `/payments` collection, the 'paymentType' and 'periodCovered' fields within each payment document provide sufficient logical segregation for client-side processing. This approach avoids creating separate physical collections for daily and monthly payments, which would introduce redundancy and potential synchronization issues, while still allowing the application logic to treat them distinctly. The new monthly payment status logic ('Waiting', 'Pending', 'Paid') is handled entirely by the client application. This is crucial for Query Authorization Principles (QAPs), as Firestore rules will simply grant authorized admins (via `/roles_admin`) full read access to `/members` and `/payments` collections. The application will then perform the necessary queries, filters (e.g., by 'paymentType', 'periodCovered'), and calculations (based on 'monthlyDueDate' and current date) on the retrieved data to determine and display the correct status. This ensures rules are not used as filters and maintain predictable performance for 'list' operations. The addition of 'monthlyDueDate' to the 'Member' entity is a simple schema extension, and the existing 'periodCovered' field in 'Payment' documents (e.g., 'YYYY-MM' for monthly) naturally supports the new logic for identifying payments within a specific month without requiring schema changes to the 'Payment' entity itself.
